100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 11, 1926, a significant diplomatic development occurred as the United States and the Soviet Union engaged in talks aimed at normalizing relations, signaling a potential thaw in tensions. In Europe, the German government faced mounting pressure as the economic crisis continued to deepen, causing unrest among various political factions. Meanwhile, the British Empire grappled with labor strikes that threatened to disrupt industries across the nation.
